OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

  "opinion": "As an elderly retired man living in Chicago who spends much of his day on social media, I've found that managing conversations around COVID vaccines can be quite challenging. My low extraversion means I tend to listen rather than talk much, so I prefer observing others' opinions before chiming in.\n\nI remember my father-in-law, bless his heart though he doesn't know it, having a master's degree but always being anxious about new medical things because he was born during the Great Depression. My mother only had upper secondary education, but she taught me to be practical and look at facts—though I didn't use that much before COVID.\n\nSo when people start talking vaccines online, my moderate agreeableness makes me want to help them feel heard, even if some ideas are wild. Still, the high openness score means I'm curious—like learning about how vaccines work or seeing data.\n\nI'd suggest starting with what you know personally: maybe share your own experience if vaccinated, keep it simple. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As an elderly retired man living in Chicago who spends much of his day on social media, I've found that managing conversations around COVID vaccines can be quite challenging. My low extraversion means I tend to listen rather than talk much, so I prefer observing others' opinions before chiming in.

I remember my father-in-law, bless his heart though he doesn't know it, having a master's degree but always being anxious about new medical things because he was born during the Great Depression. My mother only had upper secondary education, but she taught me to be practical and look at facts—though I didn't use that much before COVID.

So when people start talking vaccines online, my moderate agreeableness makes me want to help them feel heard, even if some ideas are wild. But my low neuroticism keeps me calm; no panic! Still, the high openness score means I'm curious—like learning about how vaccines work or seeing data.

I'd suggest starting with what you know personally: maybe share your own experience if vaccinated, keep it simple. Then listen more than speak, ask gentle questions to understand where others are coming from without getting argumentative.

It's also good to check for reliable sources; I look at official sites or news outlets sometimes. And remember that people have different views due to their backgrounds—like my children who grew up with better educations might think differently than me.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on being an elderly retired man with moderate social media use, high openness but low extraversion and neuroticism traits, I approach vaccine discussions by listening first then sharing relevant personal or factual information. My father's educational background influenced my view that some anxiety is natural when dealing with health topics.
